conflict: Paul confronting Tommy with the truth about the fire, the leverage, and Cliff's murder
consequence: Tommy's secret is out between them — Paul decides not to protect Tommy the way Martin did
must_land_beats: Paul confronts Tommy alone — with grief, not aggression
Martin spent twenty-two years protecting Tommy, and now Martin is dead and Cliff is dead and the protection is gone
Tommy's response: denial, then anger, then something that looks like relief at being known
Paul tells Tommy that Martin left him the house — this is the detail that breaks Tommy
The irony: the chain of obligation was also the thing holding Tommy together
Paul's ultimatum: Tommy has until morning to go to Mallick, or Paul will

payoffs_to_land.description: The subtle protectiveness seeded across the novel resolves here — Martin's loyalty to Tommy was not friendship but managed guilt and genuine, complicated love. The will, the protectiveness, the twenty-five years of making sure Tommy came back — all of it was about the fire.
Paul tells Tommy that Martin left him the house. Tommy breaks — the gift is also a sentence, proof that Martin's entire relationship with Tommy was defined by the fire.

pressure_source: Paul confronting Tommy with the truth about the fire, the leverage, and Cliff's murder
secondary_character_beats: Tommy's breakdown should not be theatrical — a man sitting on a deck realizing his life is over, not a dramatic confession
state_shift: Tommy's secret is out between them — Paul decides not to protect Tommy the way Martin did
